summary: No new Alembic migrations were added in this sprint. The migration chain is linear with a single head at n6o7p8q9r0s1 (add_push_subscriptions). The two most recent pre-sprint migrations (m5n6o7p8q9r0 redesign step 2, n6o7p8q9r0s1 push_subscriptions) both have proper downgrade() implementations. Migration m5n6o7p8q9r0 adds columns with NULL defaults and a new table — safe for hot deployment. Migration n6o7p8q9r0s1 creates push_subscriptions with CASCADE FK and correct index, fully reversible. No data-destructive operations, no missing downgrades, no branch conflicts.

findings.title: m5n6o7p8q9r0: alerts.condition type change — already deployed
findings.detail: The migration changed alerts.condition from a PostgreSQL ENUM to VARCHAR + CHECK constraint to accommodate pct_change/drawdown. This is an additive expansion and is backward compatible. Existing rows with above/below values remain valid. Already applied in production.
findings.fix: No action needed. Already deployed and in production at 39020d1.
